---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 49f992b7f272639d945ea2c6a628add2460e9cee
findings:
  - { id: judged-model-check-feature-no-storyref-variant-unchecked, kind: judged, text: "checkTemplates (cmd/verdi/model.go) justifies its variant coverage as \"every variant a real scaffold consumer can produce\" — that is why the story spike branch got its own modelCheckVariant — but modelCheckDefaultData always sets StoryRef, so the feature template's other real consumer variant is never round-tripped: `design start --kind feature` with no tracker ref passes storyRef==\"\" (05 §CLI's documented optionality, runDesignStart), driving the template's `{{if .StoryRef}}` branch the other way. A store override feature.md broken only in its empty-StoryRef path (e.g. one that emits `story: {{.StoryRef}}` unconditionally, rendering an empty scalar that fails strict decode) passes `verdi model check` clean and surfaces for the first time at someone's first ref-less `design start` — exactly the fail-at-check-time-never-at-first-use promise ac-3 makes. The embedded canonical template is proven safe in this variant at unit level (TestDecodedFieldEquivalenceToLegacy's \"feature, no story ref\" case), so the gap is confined to store overrides, but store overrides are the thing ac-3 exists to police. (confidence 0.50)" }
digest: sha256:9cb35d0de23e76b99c7515d17b21458d4bdf21ec9f53b57bb13725c592b1fd22
integrity: sha256:52226faf7d3365329ca42f3bb43342f43528ff7d6429dde2909c7b5e10c0f73c
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"model-check-feature-no-storyref-variant-unchecked\",\"text\":\"checkTemplates (cmd/verdi/model.go) justifies its variant coverage as \\\"every variant a real scaffold consumer can produce\\\" — that is why the story spike branch got its own modelCheckVariant — but modelCheckDefaultData always sets StoryRef, so the feature template's other real consumer variant is never round-tripped: `design start --kind feature` with no tracker ref passes storyRef==\\\"\\\" (05 §CLI's documented optionality, runDesignStart), driving the template's `{{if .StoryRef}}` branch the other way. A store override feature.md broken only in its empty-StoryRef path (e.g. one that emits `story: {{.StoryRef}}` unconditionally, rendering an empty scalar that fails strict decode) passes `verdi model check` clean and surfaces for the first time at someone's first ref-less `design start` — exactly the fail-at-check-time-never-at-first-use promise ac-3 makes. The embedded canonical template is proven safe in this variant at unit level (TestDecodedFieldEquivalenceToLegacy's \\\"feature, no story ref\\\" case), so the gap is confined to store overrides, but store overrides are the thing ac-3 exists to police.\",\"confidence\":0.5}]}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/scaffold-templates@49f992b7f272639d945ea2c6a628add2460e9cee, spec/scaffold-templates@5e7677b5bd7ddd0c6a7c9adc94733c37c49fc6a9], digest: sha256:9cb35d0de23e76b99c7515d17b21458d4bdf21ec9f53b57bb13725c592b1fd22, integrity: sha256:52226faf7d3365329ca42f3bb43342f43528ff7d6429dde2909c7b5e10c0f73c }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-model-check-feature-no-storyref-variant-unchecked** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: checkTemplates (cmd/verdi/model.go) justifies its variant coverage as "every variant a real scaffold consumer can produce" — that is why the story spike branch got its own modelCheckVariant — but modelCheckDefaultData always sets StoryRef, so the feature template's other real consumer variant is never round-tripped: `design start --kind feature` with no tracker ref passes storyRef=="" (05 §CLI's documented optionality, runDesignStart), driving the template's `{{if .StoryRef}}` branch the other way. A store override feature.md broken only in its empty-StoryRef path (e.g. one that emits `story: {{.StoryRef}}` unconditionally, rendering an empty scalar that fails strict decode) passes `verdi model check` clean and surfaces for the first time at someone's first ref-less `design start` — exactly the fail-at-check-time-never-at-first-use promise ac-3 makes. The embedded canonical template is proven safe in this variant at unit level (TestDecodedFieldEquivalenceToLegacy's "feature, no story ref" case), so the gap is confined to store overrides, but store overrides are the thing ac-3 exists to police. (confidence 0.50)
